properties.decision: Portal as control center, Mattermost for communication only. Monorepo with React SPA + Fastify API + separate BullMQ worker.
properties.title: Hybrid LMS + AI Platform Architecture (Variant C)
properties.rationale: Clean separation of concerns: portal handles all learning management and AI workflows, Mattermost is limited to messaging. Monorepo simplifies dependency management for a small team.

properties.decision: JWT tokens stored in httpOnly cookies instead of localStorage or Bearer tokens.
properties.title: JWT in httpOnly Cookies
properties.rationale: XSS protection (JS cannot access httpOnly cookies), SSE compatibility (cookies sent automatically), CSRF mitigated via SameSite=Strict.

properties.decision: Each student group maps to a separate Mattermost Team with standardized channel naming.
properties.title: Team per Group in Mattermost
properties.rationale: Clean isolation between groups, simple channel naming convention, trivial enrollment management via Team membership.

properties.decision: Synchronous processing for OpenRouter API + asynchronous with callback for Kie.ai.
properties.title: Two AI Processing Patterns
properties.rationale: Different AI providers have fundamentally different APIs. OpenRouter supports synchronous request-response, Kie.ai requires async job submission with webhook callback.

properties.decision: PostgreSQL pg_advisory_xact_lock for quota check-and-decrement operations.
properties.title: Advisory Locks for Quota Race Conditions
properties.rationale: Works correctly for empty tables (unlike SELECT FOR UPDATE), minimal deadlock risk, perfectly adequate for ~40 concurrent students.

properties.decision: AI model definitions stored as YAML in git repository, synced to database at application startup.
properties.title: Config-as-Seed for AI Model Registry
properties.rationale: Version control and code review for model configuration changes, while maintaining API-level access for runtime queries.

properties.decision: Fastify API and BullMQ Worker managed via pm2 process manager instead of Docker containers.
properties.title: pm2 for Backend (not Docker)
properties.rationale: Simpler updates (git pull + pm2 reload), native log management, built-in monitoring. Docker overhead unnecessary for single-server deployment.

properties.decision: Caddy chosen over nginx as the reverse proxy server.
properties.title: Caddy as Reverse Proxy
properties.rationale: Automatic HTTPS via Lets Encrypt with zero configuration, minimal Caddyfile syntax, native HTTP/2 support, automatic certificate renewal.

properties.decision: Two environments only: Development (local) and Production. No staging.
properties.title: No Staging Environment
properties.rationale: Scale of ~40 students with a single teacher does not justify staging infrastructure cost. Manual GitHub Actions deploy with confirmation gate provides sufficient safety.

properties.decision: Private MinIO bucket with all file access mediated through presigned URLs generated by the API.
properties.title: Presigned URLs for All File Access
properties.rationale: Authorization enforced at API level before URL generation. No public bucket policies needed. URLs expire, preventing link sharing abuse.
